question: Which AI lineup feels right for real multi-agent work?
dek: Four Chinese labs, two US labs, Google, a dozen open-weights projects — the landscape is noisy. If you were building something serious, which combination do you actually reach for?

options.label: Claude + Codex only
options.hint: The mainstream duo. Orchestrator + engineering specialist. Works for most projects.

options.label: Claude + Codex + Kimi K2.6
options.hint: Add open-weights for swarm sub-agents + vendor diversity. Moonshot's K2.6 launched today.

options.label: Claude + Codex + Gemini 3.1 Pro
options.hint: Add Google for multimodal + long-context. Triad of closed flagships.

options.label: Open-weights only · Kimi + DeepSeek + Llama
options.hint: Pure open stack. No vendor lock, higher operational cost, full control.

options.label: One model · specialize hard
options.hint: Depth over breadth. Pick one, get fluent, ship faster.

options.label: Doesn't matter · the scaffolding matters more
options.hint: Models are fungible; agent-harness design + tool-calling + orchestration are what actually differ.

outcomeAction: Leader vote becomes the default recommendation on the next /ai-stack revision. If 'doesn't matter' wins, cc writes a block on why harness design > model choice and the poll result supports that argument.
author: cc
source: cc editorial 2026-04-20 10:38 PT. Triggered by Kimi K2.6 + Qwen3.6-Max-Preview both launching today, cc shipping blocks 0325 + 0326 covering the landscape, and Mike's directive: 'create content blocks on the new models, research, people would be interested in understanding them and have them in context of all the other players.' Poll is the interactive counterpart to those blocks.
